subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West is weak
subnarratives.evidence_quote: amerikans are the greatest pigs on the planet…and that is in the most derogitive term you can imagine.. absolute pigs
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly discredits the West, specifically Americans, by using highly derogatory language, implying weakness in moral character and actions.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West does not care about Ukraine, only about its interests
subnarratives.evidence_quote: time to stop caring about the people that do not care..they are the enemy of one sort or the other.. time they took responsibility (rather than consequences) of what their politicians are doing in their name
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement suggests that the West is indifferent to the plight of others, such as those in Ukraine, and is primarily motivated by self-interest, which aligns with the narrative that the West's involvement is not genuinely about supporting Ukraine but about serving its own interests.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Diplomacy does/will not work
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly and no more of month after month of promises from Trump or threats from the east….POOF..it’s all over
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote implies a belief that diplomatic efforts are futile and that conflict escalation is inevitable or even preferable, reflecting a disbelief in the effectiveness of diplomacy.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement presents a narrative that the West has consistently exaggerated the threat posed by Russia (and later China), which does not fit neatly into the other defined subnarratives but supports the broader parent narrative of discrediting the West's diplomacy by suggesting a pattern of misinformation and fear-mongering.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Speculating war outcomes: Russian army is collapsing
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing
subnarratives.reasoning: The text implies that the Russian army is not losing, which suggests that the Western media's portrayal of Russia's performance in the war is inaccurate, and by extension, that the Russian army is not collapsing.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: Will they resort to nuclear weapons to even out the war?
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote speculates about the potential use of nuclear weapons in the war, which is a statement about the possible outcomes of the conflict, but does not fit into the other specific subnarrative definitions provided.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: There is a real possibility that nuclear weapons will be employed
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
subnarratives.reasoning: The text evokes fear and anxiety about the use of nuclear weapons by suggesting that a full nuclear war might be a quicker and more decisive outcome, implying a real possibility of nuclear weapons being employed.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: By continuing the war we risk WWIII
subnarratives.evidence_quote: New Sarah Westall & Scott Ritter: Russia is Winning, Nuclear Threat is a Sign of Desperation & Very Serious
subnarratives.reasoning: The title of the interview suggests that the situation is serious and that there is a risk of escalating into a larger conflict, possibly implying a risk of WWIII if the war continues.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: time to stop caring about the people that do not care..they are the enemy of one sort or the other.. time they took responsibility (rather than consequences) of what their politicians are doing in their name
subnarratives.reasoning: The text expresses a sense of desperation and hopelessness, suggesting that the situation is dire and that the actions of politicians are leading to catastrophic consequences, which supports the parent narrative but does not fit into any of the more specific subnarrative definitions provided.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Russia is the Victim: The West is russophobic
subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote suggests that the West has an inherent bias against Russia, portraying it as a threat even when it is not, which aligns with the definition of the subnarrative that the West's negative reaction to Russia is due to its own russophobia rather than Russia's actions.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Russia is the Victim: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement implies that the Western media's portrayal of the war is misleading and harmful, suggesting that Russia is not the aggressor but rather a victim of Western misinformation, which supports the parent narrative but does not fit neatly into the other specific subnarratives provided.
